mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth above 150k per month signals robust labor market tightening, which increases inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward as investors demand higher real returns to compensate for expected price pressures over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134825k as of Jan 2013 shows continued post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with accelerating labor demand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines or remains flat in > 55% of 20-day windows following months with PAYEMS growth > 150k, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (0.14% as of Jan 2013) constrains further monetary accommodation, signaling that the Fed has exhausted conventional policy tools. This regime shift causes investors to anticipate eventual rate normalization, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as the market prices in future tightening over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 6+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% indicates zero lower bound constraint
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary regime with forward guidance expectations
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 60% of triggered instances, or yield change shows negative correlation with FEDFUNDS level (Spearman rho < -0.10) over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 954k units signal strong residential investment demand, which increases aggregate demand and inflation expectations. This demand pressure causes the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors require higher yields to hold longer-duration bonds in an inflationary environment over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 900k units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 954k as of Dec 2012 shows housing recovery acceleration
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with rising housing demand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines when HOUST > 900k in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC between HOUST and yield change is < 0.04 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) signals supply-constrained expansion. When supply cannot keep pace with demand, inflation accelerates, forcing the Fed to signal future rate increases and pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as investors reprice inflation risk over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.5% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.62 and CPIAUCSL at 230.98 as of Jan 2013
regime_hint: supply-constrained recovery with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 55% of instances where both trigger conditions are met, or the joint effect of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL shows negative correlation with yield change (Spearman rho < -0.08) over rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) momentum above 416 billion dollars indicates strong consumer demand and economic growth expectations. This demand signal causes investors to revise growth expectations upward, increasing the term premium and pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as investors demand compensation for longer duration exposure over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 416633 million as of Jan 2013 shows sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 60% of months with RSAFS > 416 billion, or Rank IC between RSAFS and yield change is < 0.03 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 7.9% remains elevated relative to pre-crisis levels, signaling slack in the labor market. This slack constrains wage growth and inflation expectations, causing investors to expect the Fed to maintain accommodative policy longer, pushing 10-year Treasury yields lower as investors accept lower real returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains above 7.5% for 3+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.9% as of Jan 2013 indicates persistent labor market slack
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with elevated unemployment and policy accommodation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of 60-day windows following months with UNRATE > 7.5%, or Rank IC between UNRATE and yield change is > 0.05 (positive correlation) over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) at 13647.6 billion chained 2017 dollars combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE < 8.0%) signals late-cycle expansion momentum. This combination triggers expectations of Fed tightening within 12-18 months, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors front-run policy normalization over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% AND UNRATE declines > 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13647.6B and UNRATE at 7.9% as of Q4 2012
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with accelerating growth and tightening labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 60% of 60-day windows following quarters with both conditions met, or the interaction effect (GDPC1 × UNRATE change) shows negative correlation with yield change (Spearman rho < -0.10) over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is constrained at zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) and inflation expectations remain anchored (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2.5%), the real policy rate becomes deeply negative. This regime causes investors to demand higher nominal yields on longer-duration bonds to escape negative real returns, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth between 1.5% and 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% and CPIAUCSL at 230.98 as of Jan 2013
regime_hint: zero lower bound with anchored inflation expectations and negative real rates
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of 5-day windows following months where both trigger conditions are met, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL) shows negative correlation with yield change (Spearman rho < -0.12) over any 12-month rolling window.
